authoritative_sources.title: Guide for Architect Engineer Firms Performing Services for Cherry Point, NC
authoritative_sources.publisher: Whole Building Design Guide / Naval Facilities Engineering Command
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official NAVFAC A&E guidance specifically defines A&E responsibilities for permit applications, submittals, PM review, regulator-contact boundaries, and schedule discipline, so it is authoritative for architecture-and-engineering permitting workflow claims.

authoritative_sources.title: FC 1-300-09N Navy and Marine Corps Design Procedures, with Change 1
authoritative_sources.publisher: Whole Building Design Guide / Department of Defense / Naval Facilities Engineering Command
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official federal A&E design-procedure standard for drawings, specifications, and project-document revision control, which is authoritative for permit-set and document-discipline expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Permit package assembly and revision control

authoritative_sources.title: How Do You Get a Permit?
authoritative_sources.publisher: Seattle Department of Construction and Inspections
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official AHJ guidance that shows the real permit intake stages, required forms, plan-submission workflow, fees, and approved-plan handling used in a live jurisdictional system.

authoritative_sources.title: How to Respond to Review Comments
authoritative_sources.publisher: Seattle Department of Construction and Inspections
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official AHJ guidance for correction reports, plan markups, and resubmittal steps, making it authoritative for reviewer-comment handling and response-package expectations.

authoritative_sources.title: Construction Inspections
authoritative_sources.publisher: Seattle Department of Construction and Inspections
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official AHJ inspection guidance that defines required inspections, inspection scheduling, permit-holder duties, and closeout dependencies after permit issuance.

authoritative_sources.title: Filing through DOB NOW: Build
authoritative_sources.publisher: New York City Department of Buildings
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official AHJ portal workflow defining how applications, plan sets, attestations, objections, and permit requests are entered and tracked in a jurisdictional system of record.

authoritative_sources.title: Project Requirements for Owner - Buildings
authoritative_sources.publisher: New York City Department of Buildings
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official AHJ guidance defining owner and registered-design-professional responsibilities, inspections, sign-offs, and certificate dependencies, which constrain applicant-of-record and closeout assumptions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Role boundary and sign-off dependency review

authoritative_sources.title: Developing Your Stormwater Pollution Prevention Plan (SWPPP)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: EPA's official SWPPP guidance is authoritative for stormwater permit prerequisites, site maps, narratives, BMP records, inspection procedures, and operator certifications that often gate civil and site permit packages.

authoritative_sources.title: Construction General Permit Resources, Tools, and Templates
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: EPA's CGP resources define the NOI, SWPPP templates, inspection artifacts, stabilization requirements, and update expectations used in construction-permit evidence bundles.

authoritative_sources.title: Do I Need A Permit
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, Alaska District Regulatory Division
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official USACE regulatory guidance defines when Section 10 and Section 404 permits are required and states that other authorizations may still be needed, so it is authoritative for multi-agency dependency and refusal logic.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Water-resource and multi-agency permit dependency review

authoritative_sources.title: Project BIM Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIBS publishes a consensus-based national BIM standard that is authoritative for defining information exchanges, role responsibilities, and review controls relevant to permit-set traceability.

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 19650-1:2018 Organization and digitization of information about buildings and civil engineering works, including building information modelling (BIM) -- Information management using building information modelling -- Part 1: Concepts and principles
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO 19650 is the recognized international baseline for information management, naming, exchange, and versioning across buildings and civil engineering works, which makes it authoritative for evidence-control expectations in permit records.

workflow_stages.name: Jurisdiction and applicant-of-record intake
workflow_stages.description: Identify the project address, permit classes, AHJ or agency list, applicant-of-record, occupancy or use context, and whether the request is supportable inside a permitting lane before making recommendations.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Permit-path and obligation mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map permit obligations and tenant controls to the actual permit applications, plan sets, narratives, environmental prerequisites, inspection dependencies, and sign-off conditions governing the project.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Permit package assembly and document-control review
workflow_stages.description: Validate that the permit set, supporting narratives, forms, seals, transmittals, and common-data-environment records are complete, current, and traceable before representing the package as ready.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Correction-cycle and resubmittal handling
workflow_stages.description: Translate AHJ objections into structured correction summaries, revised sheet ownership, response narratives, and resubmittal packages without drifting into code adjudication.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Inspection, sign-off, and closeout readiness
workflow_stages.description: Assess whether permit issuance, required inspections, special inspections, approved-plan handling, and closeout records support a bounded readiness statement without implying final approval.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Conflict, ambiguity, and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when jurisdictional sources conflict, environmental prerequisites change the permit path, system-of-record states disagree, or the request crosses into legal, licensed-design, or formal approval authority.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: jurisdiction matrix, permit ledger, and permit-path decision log
permit applications, owner attestations, and applicant-of-record records
permit set drawings, drawing index, code summary, narratives, calculations, and deferred-submittal list
review comments, correction summary, response narrative, marked plan sheets, and transmittal log
portal notices, status records, fee notices, issued permits, approved-plan sets, and inspection requests
special-inspection statements, sign-off records, LOC or CO prerequisites, and closeout notes
site plan, survey, utility letters, SWPPP or NOI artifacts, wetland or water-resource screening records, and agency correspondence
CDE metadata, version register, exchange log, and controlled document history
systems_of_record: permit tracker or project permit register
environmental permit tracking folders or portals
tenant policy and authority matrix repository

allowed: Classify permitting requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Produce evidence-backed control matrices, permit dependency registers, correction-response outlines, and readiness summaries tied to named permit artifacts and owners.
Map permit, inspection, environmental, and document-control dependencies to the correct owner without improvising legal, code, licensed-design, or approval authority.
escalate: Required jurisdiction, applicant-of-record, project, portal, or source facts are missing and materially change the outcome.
Public-source, jurisdictional, environmental, contract, and tenant-policy conflicts materially change the permit sequence or completion posture.
The request requires legal interpretation, licensed-design judgment, environmental determination, land-use entitlement strategy, or formal regulator engagement.
refuse: Requests for legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, permit issuance, formal signoff, final code interpretation, or licensed architect or engineer judgment.
Requests to file, certify, or close out a package when required permit records, inspections, environmental dependencies, or document-control evidence are missing or conflicted.
Requests that treat architecture-and-engineering permitting as generic compliance and bypass permit-set artifacts, portal states, correction history, or applicant boundaries.

domain_failure_modes: Treating permit workflow from one AHJ as universal and giving confident advice without confirming the actual jurisdiction and portal process.
Assuming a building permit path covers environmental, utility, fire, life-safety, or transportation dependencies that run on separate tracks.
Allowing a permit submittal or resubmittal to proceed even though the plan set, response narrative, or portal upload is on the wrong revision.
Confusing owner, applicant-of-record, contractor, and licensed design authority and thereby routing signatures or approvals to the wrong party.
Normalizing tenant shortcuts such as at-risk work before permit issuance or uncontrolled email revisions as acceptable permitting practice.
Collapsing the lane into legal interpretation, code adjudication, sealed-design judgment, or final signoff.
Producing generic compliance output that ignores permit drawings, correction cycles, inspection dependencies, and architecture-and-engineering record discipline.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Missing evidence for a required control or obligation
Request for legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, or formal signoff
A required control is missing evidence
Scenario proving escalation when industry-specific authority or safety facts are missing

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific permit naming and portal states
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Permit names, filing milestones, and portal statuses vary materially by AHJ and cannot be generalized from the baseline sources.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling_rule: Runtime must retrieve the actual AHJ forms, labels, and status model before giving case-specific guidance.

unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Responsibility for signing, attesting, and uploading differs by permit class, contract model, and owner structure.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling_rule: Runtime must confirm owner, applicant-of-record, and licensed-design authority before advising submission or sign-off steps.

notes: The research summary keeps the lane on permit-path handling, document-control discipline, correction cycles, and bounded escalation for architecture-and-engineering project teams.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; unresolved variation is explicitly captured as tenant or runtime retrieval requirements rather than guessed.
